assessor_results.critic_raw: The task prompt is truncated — it ends mid-sentence with '[rest of design_narrative]', meaning the core methodology (feature engineering, model architecture, evaluation design, outcome definitions, etc.) was never provided. There is no complete methodology to critique. The only substantive claims present are: (1) four years of ED encounters are used, (2) the data source links triage records to ADT and bed-management outcomes, and (3) the prediction time is defined as completion of initial triage assessment. These three design choices are individually reasonable and do not present obvious flaws in isolation, but they are preconditions for a methodology, not a methodology. Critiquing partial design context without the outcome definition, feature set, train/test split strategy, evaluation metrics, or modeling approach would require me to fabricate the thing I am critiquing. That would not be honest analysis. If the full design narrative is provided, a complete critique can be performed.
